meaning: n.替代,用处,好处v.对...有利,对...有用
examples: You shall undertake this trip and visit Heidi in our stead.
To wit, that a spurious Prince of Wales was being feasted by the city in his stead.
Foxton was dismissed and John Smith was appointed in his stead.
Stead pleaded guilty to five counts of fraud and will be sentenced on 21 June.
And Stead wrapped up a vital victory with a brilliant solo effort 12 minutes from time.
